design_philosophy.core_loop: Select wrestler → train between matches via button-mashing exercises → compete in sequential single or multiplayer wrestling matches → advance through roster of opponents → face final boss (Blue King)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management of training spheres (damage multiplier) versus accumulated match losses that deplete them; momentum swings in grapple situations determine combat advantage
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become an international professional wrestling champion by mastering diverse move sets and training regimens; engage in televisual wrestling spectacle with dynamic action replays
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: First wrestling game with play-by-play announcer commentary (Tom Talker) and action replay cutscenes mimicking broadcast television; grapple-initiated combat system (no dedicated grapple button) creates situational move variety; training sphere mechanic ties progression directly to performance consequences

difficulty.lives_system: Training sphere system: 7 spheres maximum; losing a match costs one sphere; must retrain to recover; when depleted, cannot progress until spheres are rebuilt
difficulty.continues_system: Single-player campaign allows continued play after losses with sphere penalty; Japanese version uses password system for save progression
difficulty.checkpointing: Password system in Japanese version enables mid-campaign saves; US/EU versions require continuous play through entire roster
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: player controls training intensity and frequency between matches; can grind training spheres to maximize damage output before facing difficult opponents; sphere loss on defeat creates meaningful failure states

progression.upgrade_system: Training sphere accumulation system: 7-sphere cap; player performs button-mashing exercises (squats, situps, push-ups) to fill progress meter and gain spheres; spheres increase move damage; lost on match defeat; sumo wrestler variant adds cosmetic difficulty to training
progression.unlock_mechanism: Defeat all 10 playable wrestlers in sequence to unlock and face final boss Blue King (The Earl of Doom, non-playable); wrestler selection determines opponent order but all must be defeated
replayability_hooks: 10 distinct playable wrestlers with unique move sets (20+ moves per wrestler)
Two-player versus mode with momentum meter mechanics
Different specialty moves per wrestler encourage replaying with alternate characters
Training sphere system creates variable difficulty runs based on training discipline
Password system (Japanese version) enables multiple save files

world_building_techniques: International wrestler roster with geographic origins (Japan, Mexico, Greece, UK, Hong Kong, Soviet Union, West Germany, Ecuador, Venezuela, USA) establishing global wrestling federation
Wrestler nicknames and specialties suggest distinct personas and fighting styles
Tom Talker (animated announcer character) provides color commentary establishing television broadcast atmosphere
Action replay cutscenes for signature moves create cinematic moments within arcade gameplay
Non-playable final boss (Blue King / Earl of Doom) positions him as ultimate antagonist

boss_design.philosophy: Single climactic non-playable final boss encounter after defeating entire playable roster; represents apex challenge and narrative culmination
notable_bosses: Blue King (The Earl of Doom) - final boss character, non-playable, encountered only after defeating all 10 roster wrestlers

faction_indicators: International wrestler nationalities create implicit geopolitical faction system (East vs. West through Soviet/German/UK wrestlers; Latin American presence through Ecuador/Venezuela/Mexico wrestlers; Asian representation through Japan/Hong Kong)
Wrestler specialties and moves suggest distinct training philosophies and fighting cultures
danger_signals: Blue King as 'non-playable final boss' positioned outside normal wrestler hierarchy, suggesting authoritarian/exceptional threat status
Earl of Doom title implies existential threat to legitimate wrestling order
environmental_storytelling: Wrestling ring as competitive arena for international athletes
Training facility (between-match training sequences) suggests professional wrestling infrastructure
Broadcaster presence (Tom Talker announcer) implies sport legitimacy and spectacle

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Tecmo World Wrestling page)
